Member-only story

8 min read
1 hour ago

--

Part 2 of 2 — The Payoff at Scale, the Fine Print, and the Horizon
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Compact Object HeadersIn Part 1 we watched a single Point drop from 24 bytes to 16 by flipping -XX:+UseCompactObjectHeaders — a third of the object gone, the class pointer folded into the mark word, no code changed. And I told you the truth: one object is a rounding error. Now let's see whether a third off every header actually matters when there are forty million of them, what it does to your cloud bill, and — because I promised honesty before enthusiasm — every place where the shine comes off.
Does it actually move the needle? The numbers say yes
Here’s the part that turns a cute micro-optimization into a line item. The published, independently-reproduced benchmark results are genuinely striking for a change that asks nothing of your code:

• On SPECjbb2015, enabling compact headers gave back roughly 22% of heap space and about 8% of CPU time, and cut the number of garbage-collection cycles by around 15% — on both G1 and Parallel GC.

• A straightforward benchmark allocating ten million Point objects on JDK 25 measured up to ~30% less committed memory and around 12% less used memory once compact headers were on.

• And across real production applications, the Lilliput team’s…
